text:
Once upon a matchday — it was the quarter-final, on the 11th of July 2026 — two teams walked out under the soft evening lights. Billed as Haaland versus Kane, it was Jude Bellingham who decided it — and ended Norway's fairytale. Two of the tournament's most feared number nines shared the marquee, but the headlines belonged to someone else entirely.

In the 36th minute, Andreas Schjelderup sent the ball gently home, and the score whispered 1–0. In the 47th minute, Jude Bellingham sent the ball gently home, and the score whispered 1–1. In the 93rd minute, Jude Bellingham sent the ball gently home, and the score whispered 1–2.

Andreas Schjelderup gave Norway a deserved lead late in the first half, and the dream of a first semi-final flickered. Bellingham levelled in first-half stoppage time with a driving run and a cool finish. He struck again in extra time to send England to their first semi-final since 2018 and bring Erling Haaland's remarkable run to a shocking end. Neither Haaland nor Kane found the net; the game's biggest stage found a different hero.

And so the story ended Norway 1–2 England. England carried on their long journey, and Norway lay down to rest. Every goal in this tale is a true one — kept safe and provable on the blockchain by TxLINE — so you can close your eyes knowing not a single fact was ever made up. Sleep well, and goodnight.
